$schema_note: GUARD B — RETIRED-DOCTRINE RESIDUAL LINT registry. SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for doctrine VALUES that have been retired and must never silently re-appear in the Presentations tree. scripts/doctrine_residual_check.py greps each entry's `pattern` (regex, IGNORECASE+MULTILINE) over the files matched by `scope` (glob, relative to the presentations/ root). Any match on a line/block that does NOT also carry an allowed_context_marker (an explicit retirement/replacement signal) is an OFFENDER and fails the check non-zero. A retired value is allowed to survive ONLY as documented history — i.e. on a line (or within `context_window` lines) that says it was retired/replaced. RETIRING A DOCTRINE VALUE IS A CONTRACT: add it here (Guard B) so the OLD value can never quietly creep back in as a live instruction. To add a new retired pattern: append an object with a unique `id`, a `pattern` that matches the retired value, a `scope` glob, a one-line `why`, and (optionally) per-pattern `allowed_context_markers` (otherwise the registry-level defaults apply).

patterns.why: The retired '>= 7 / sung at least 7 times / seven times' HOOK FLOOR produced the reference-case 40-slide footer-stamping. The live gate is the banded AF-HOOK CEILING (3-4 dedicated slides). The old floor must never re-appear as a live instruction.
